messages.subject: Sainsbury's ops team demo + Woolworths case study
messages.body:
Kieran,

Great session last week — the 200-of-10,000 framing was exactly the kind of specificity that helps us make the case internally too.

Attaching the Woolworths AU case study — they ran Genesys + Salesforce exactly as you do, and the QA automation went live within 8 weeks. The ops team comparison will be useful context.

On the ops team demo — I can flex to whatever format works. Happy to come in or run it remotely. What does the week of [date] look like?

Marcus

talk_track.opener: Hi [name], it's Marcus from Cresta — we spoke with Kieran and Viswa last week about the QA gap. Kieran mentioned you'd be involved in the ops team review. Do you have 2 minutes?
talk_track.pitch: We work with retailers like Woolworths who run Genesys and Salesforce just like you. We sit on top of both and give you 100% call QA — replacing the 2% manual sample — plus real-time agent guidance. No rip-and-replace, 8 weeks to live.
talk_track.qualifying_question: Kieran mentioned the ops team has a view on QA prioritisation — is 100% coverage something you've been asked to solve, or is it more about agent coaching and consistency?
talk_track.ask: We've done the demo with Kieran and Viswa — can I bring that to the broader ops team? 90 minutes, on-site or remote.

objections.objection: We already have Salesforce Agentforce for this
objections.response: Agentforce is brilliant for CRM automation — it's not built for contact centres. Cresta is AI-native for CC: 100% QM on every call, real-time agent guidance in the moment. Forrester named us #1. We sit alongside Agentforce, not against it.

objections.objection: The data procurement process takes 3 months
objections.response: We don't need a data dump to start. We go live on real calls from week one and build the business case from actual interaction data. Woolworths did exactly this.

objections.objection: We're mid-strategy — timing isn't right
objections.response: Viswa said the 3-year strategy is being defined right now. That's exactly the right time to set the CC AI standard — not retrofit it in year two.

messages.subject: Amazon Connect + Salesforce at HL — Michael Loberman suggested you
messages.body:
Charlie,

Michael Loberman suggested you're the right person — you own the Amazon Connect and Salesforce roadmap at HL.

Two things I'd like your view on:

1. Is Agentforce delivering the CC intelligence you need for agent coaching — or is it still primarily a CRM tool?

2. Under FCA Consumer Duty, how are you evidencing fair outcomes across pensions and investment conversations at scale? Manual QA on a sample doesn't evidence 100% — which is what the Duty requires.

We work with Mutual of Omaha on Amazon Connect — same stack, financial services, similar regulatory environment. 33% improvement in outcomes, QA cost halved in 6 months. I'd also mention that Declan Hourican will recognise our name from his time at TSB.

15 minutes? Happy to come to Bristol.

Marcus

talk_track.opener: Hi Charlie, Marcus Nolan from Cresta — Michael Loberman suggested you're the right person for a conversation about the Amazon Connect and Salesforce roadmap. Do you have 2 minutes?
talk_track.pitch: We sit on top of Amazon Connect and Salesforce — no replacement — and do two things: 100% call quality coverage for FCA Consumer Duty, and real-time guidance for agents during complex investment conversations. Mutual of Omaha runs exactly your stack — 33% improvement in outcomes in 6 months.
talk_track.qualifying_question: You're in the middle of the Amazon Connect transformation — is the AI coaching layer on the roadmap for this phase, or is it a separate workstream?
talk_track.ask: 20 minutes with you and whoever owns QA and compliance monitoring? I'll bring the Mutual of Omaha case study and a specific Consumer Duty framework. Happy to come to Bristol.

messages.body:
[Name],

Hastings is growing impressively — 4.4M policies, 15% YoY, and your mobile app is clearly working. The calls still reaching your agents are the ones the app can't solve: complex claims, disputed renewals, vulnerable customers.

Under FCA Consumer Duty, you need to evidence fair outcomes on all of those — not a sample. With Genesys + Verint, you're reviewing somewhere between 5-10% post-hoc.

We work with insurers running Genesys who've solved this — 100% call QM, real-time agent guidance, no rip-and-replace.

Would 20 minutes be worthwhile?

Marcus Nolan, Cresta

messages.subject: Hyperoptic CX — a question about your Trustpilot consistency
messages.body:
Emma,

I've been looking at Hyperoptic's Trustpilot reviews in detail — the headline score is strong. But the pattern in the corpus is interesting: when interactions go well, customers name a specific agent by name. When they go badly, they describe confusion, long wait times, and agents who couldn't resolve without escalation.

That gap between your best and average agents is probably costing you thousands of customers in churn you can't currently see — especially with Openreach scaling you into a million new homes this year, with a smaller team than six months ago.

Is that a fair characterisation of what you're seeing internally?

Cox Communications — same sector, similar subscriber scaling challenge — achieved a 20% revenue increase and 28.5% reduction in handle time.

Worth 30 minutes?

Marcus Nolan, Cresta

talk_track.opener: Hi Emma, Marcus Nolan from Cresta. I sent you a note about the Trustpilot consistency gap at Hyperoptic. Do you have 2 minutes?
talk_track.pitch: We work with telecoms businesses on the gap between best and average CC agents. Cox Communications — same sector, same subscriber scaling challenge — achieved 20% revenue increase and 50% ramp time reduction.
talk_track.qualifying_question: With Openreach going live this year — what's your plan for maintaining CX consistency as subscriber volume increases but headcount has come down?
talk_track.ask: 30 minutes — not a demo, a conversation about how other telecoms businesses have scaled CX without scaling headcount.

objections.objection: Budget is tight after the redundancies
objections.response: That's exactly the context where Cresta pays back fastest. Fewer people + more customers = remaining team needs to be better, faster, more consistent. AHT + ramp time + churn reduction = the ROI.

messages.subject: AIB + Abi — the next question for your contact centre
messages.body:
Niamh,

AIB's launch of Abi is exactly the right first step — 130,000 customers self-serving is a meaningful result.

The next question every bank at your stage faces: when Abi hands off to a human agent, what guides that agent? And how do you evidence CBI-compliant outcomes on 100% of those handoffs?

We work with banks on exactly that layer — real-time agent guidance and 100% call quality assurance on top of existing Cisco infrastructure. No replacement required.

I'm from Dublin originally and I'd love 20 minutes. Happy to come to Molesworth Street.

Marcus Nolan, Cresta

talk_track.opener: Hi Niamh, Marcus Nolan from Cresta — I'm originally from Dublin. I've been following AIB's digital transformation — the Abi launch is impressive. I wanted to ask about the next layer. Do you have 2 minutes?
talk_track.pitch: Abi handles self-service brilliantly. The challenge: when Abi hands off to a human agent, what guides that agent in real-time? And how do you evidence CBI-compliant outcomes on 100% of those conversations? That's Cresta — on top of your existing Cisco, no rip-and-replace. TSB in the UK is a customer.
talk_track.qualifying_question: With 3.35M customers and Abi rolling out — is the human agent coaching layer on the roadmap for this year?
talk_track.ask: 20 minutes — in person at Molesworth Street or on a call. I'll bring the TSB reference and a CBI Consumer Protection framework.

objections.objection: We already have Abi for AI in the CC
objections.response: Abi is a virtual agent for self-service. Cresta is an agent coaching tool — it guides your human agents in real-time when Abi hands off. Complementary, not competitive.

objections.objection: We run Cisco UCCE — changing is a big project
objections.response: Cresta sits on top of Cisco UCCE — we don't replace it. API-based integration, typically 6-8 weeks. No rip-and-replace.

messages.subject: N Brown — JD Sports + Cox case studies, and which brand first?
messages.body:
Chris,

Apologies for the slight delay — as promised, JD Sports and Cox case studies are attached.

Based on what Simon raised in the demo, the two strongest immediate use cases for N Brown are:

1. AI Agent for WISMO and account queries — measurable containment from week one.
2. 100% auto-QM on FS arm interactions — closing the Consumer Duty gap that NICE's sampling leaves open.

I'd love a focused 45-minute 2nd session with you, Simon, and whoever owns FS compliance.

Of the three brands — which would benefit most from AI-assisted deflection first?

Marcus

messages.subject: Saga CC — Lean Six Sigma tells you what happened. Cresta changes what happens.
messages.body:
Sacha,

Lean Six Sigma is brilliant for understanding process variance after the fact. What it can't do is change agent behaviour in the moment it matters.

Saga's contact centre handles over-50s customers on insurance, travel, and financial queries — some of the most complex, emotionally-significant conversations in any CC. Qfiniti gives you a view of a sample of those interactions. Cresta gives you real-time guidance on all of them, and 100% QM evidence for FCA Consumer Duty.

I've been speaking with colleagues of yours who've given me a candid picture of where the CC strengths and gaps are — I think there's a meaningful conversation here.

20 minutes?

Marcus Nolan, Cresta

messages.subject: N/A — phone call
messages.body: "Nigel — Marcus Nolan from Cresta. I sent you two emails recently about the CC gaps Kate Taphouse described around the Middle East disruption and Saga's NPS as a board KPI. I know email isn't always the right medium. Do you have 2 minutes?"

talk_track.opener: Hi Sacha — Marcus Nolan from Cresta. I work with COOs who have a Lean Six Sigma background on a specific problem: how do you improve CC process consistency in real-time, not just understand it after the fact? Do you have 2 minutes?
talk_track.pitch: Qfiniti tells you what happened on a sample of calls. Cresta changes what happens in the moment — for every agent, every over-50s customer, every travel query or insurance claim. 100% QM for FCA Consumer Duty, real-time agent guidance, and the coaching insights your Lean Six Sigma background will know how to act on.
talk_track.qualifying_question: When you review Qfiniti data — what's the most common coaching intervention you end up making, and how long does it take between the call happening and the coaching being delivered?
talk_track.ask: 20 minutes — I'll bring specific data on how comparable insurers have used this to drive measurable NPS improvement, which I know is a board KPI for Saga. Happy to come to Folkestone.

objections.objection: We already have Qfiniti for QM
objections.response: Qfiniti gives you post-call analytics on a sample. Cresta gives you 100% QM in real-time AND the coaching insights Qfiniti generates — but on every call, not a subset. They're complementary. Many of our customers run both.

objections.objection: Our contact centre handles very sensitive calls — over-50s customers
objections.response: That's exactly why this matters most. Over-50s customers discussing travel insurance, pensions, equity release — those are emotionally significant conversations where empathy and accuracy both matter. Cresta ensures consistent quality on every one of them, not just the ones someone happens to review.

objections.objection: We're mid-transformation — capacity is limited
objections.response: Understood. A Lean Six Sigma background tells me you know the cost of not fixing a process while you're busy fixing other things. The implementation is 8 weeks, and the ROI shows up in the first quarter.

messages.subject: UKPN + Cresta — June 3 prep and pre-reads
messages.body:
Alex,

Looking forward to June 3 on Teams. A few things to make the most of the time:

I've attached three case studies as pre-reading — each relevant to a specific aspect of UKPN's situation:

1. United Airlines — eliminated 6M calls annually by fixing the root causes of misdirected contacts (directly relevant to what you mentioned on May 15)
2. Alaska Airlines — storm-day response CC management (outage-day CC surge analogue)
3. Brinks Home — on-premise infrastructure, no rip-and-replace, NPS +30pts

For June 3 — is there anything specific you'd like us to focus on? Happy to tailor the demo around the misdirected contacts problem specifically.

Marcus

talk_track.opener: Hi Alex — Marcus Nolan from Cresta, following up ahead of June 3. I wanted to confirm the agenda and make sure we cover the misdirected contacts problem you raised on May 15. Do you have 2 minutes?
talk_track.pitch: The misdirected contact problem is exactly what Cresta solves at the system level — real-time agent guidance that ensures every call is routed correctly from the first interaction, and conversation intelligence that identifies root causes so you can fix the process, not just the symptom. United Airlines eliminated 6 million calls annually this way.
talk_track.qualifying_question: On May 15 you mentioned misdirected contacts — how are you currently tracking that problem, and do you have a sense of the volume?
talk_track.ask: June 3 Teams — 90 minutes with you, Jenny, and Clive. We'll demo specifically around the misdirected contacts and multi-CRM cognitive load scenarios. Does that work?

objections.objection: We're a DNO — our CC needs are very specific to network operations
objections.response: That's exactly why Alaska Airlines and United Airlines are in your pre-reads — not because they're identical, but because managing surge volume around disruption events is the same underlying problem. The Alaska Airlines storm-day scenario is the closest analogue to UKPN's outage-day CC challenge.

objections.objection: We use 8x8 and Verint already — we have analytics covered
objections.response: Verint is great for post-call analytics on a sample. Cresta sits on top of your existing stack and gives you real-time guidance during the call AND 100% QM coverage — not just the calls someone reviews. The multi-CRM consolidation (Salesforce + SAP + Sage) is the immediate agent experience win.

objections.objection: Engie (parent) may have group-level vendor preferences
objections.response: Understood. UKPN has historically made CC technology decisions at the UK entity level — which is what makes this conversation appropriate at your level. A UK pilot with documented ROI is the cleanest path through any group governance process.

messages.subject: Asda + Cresta — you've seen CC AI at scale at Microsoft and Barclays
messages.body:

messages.subject: Energia + Salesforce — the intelligence layer for customer conversations
messages.body:
Phillip,

You already have Salesforce — Cresta is the intelligence layer that makes every customer conversation visible, scoreable, and improvable on your existing stack.

For energy businesses handling billing queries, outage complaints, and switching conversations, the gap between what Salesforce records and what actually happens in the call is where most CX problems hide.

As an Irish person working in the UK, I've seen how Irish energy companies approach the AI question very differently to UK players — and Energia is well-placed to lead on this.

Worth 20 minutes?

Marcus Nolan, Cresta

talk_track.opener: Hi Phillip — Marcus Nolan from Cresta. I'm originally from Dublin, so I hope you'll forgive the slightly personal cold call. You already have Salesforce — we add the intelligence layer on top. Do you have 2 minutes?
talk_track.pitch: Cresta sits on top of Salesforce and gives you 100% visibility into customer conversations — what's being said, how well it's handled, what needs to change. For an energy business with billing, outage, and switching queries, that intelligence is transformative.
talk_track.qualifying_question: How many agents does Energia have across ROI and NI — and what's the primary driver of CC contact volume right now?
talk_track.ask: 20 minutes to show you how this works on your existing Salesforce stack. Happy to come to Dublin.

objections.objection: We're regulated by CRU and Utility Regulator — procurement is complex
objections.response: Understood. Cresta is FCA and equivalent-regulated-sector compliant. The regulatory complexity is exactly why 100% call quality visibility matters more in energy than in most sectors.

objections.objection: We don't know if our CC is large enough
objections.response: That's the right question. If you have 50+ agents handling billing, outage, and switching queries, the ROI works. Worth a 20-minute call to find out.

messages.subject: Staysure + Cresta — every over-50s travel conversation is high-stakes
messages.body:
Hi [Name],

Over-50s customers with pre-existing conditions asking about travel insurance are having some of the most complex, emotionally significant conversations that reach a contact centre.

Getting those conversations right — the medical screening questions, the policy exclusion explanations, the empathy when a claim is declined — requires both specialist knowledge and consistent human judgement.

We work with insurers on exactly this: ensuring every agent has the right knowledge at the right moment, and every conversation meets FCA Consumer Duty standards.

Worth 20 minutes?

Marcus Nolan, Cresta

talk_track.opener: Hi [name] — Marcus Nolan from Cresta. I work with FCA-regulated insurers on the specialist knowledge and empathy challenge in contact centres. Staysure's over-50s, pre-existing conditions model is a particularly interesting one. Do you have 2 minutes?
talk_track.pitch: Every call to Staysure potentially involves a customer asking about cover for a serious medical condition. Cresta ensures every agent has the right policy knowledge in the moment, handles the conversation with appropriate empathy, and meets FCA Consumer Duty standards on 100% of interactions — not just the ones someone reviews.
talk_track.qualifying_question: When a customer asks about a complex pre-existing condition — how do your agents access the policy knowledge they need in real-time?
talk_track.ask: 20 minutes to show you how comparable over-50s insurers are approaching this. Happy to come to Northampton.

objections.objection: We already have Freespee for call analytics
objections.response: Freespee records and analyses calls — it's a great data source. Cresta guides agents in real-time during the call and scores 100% of interactions for FCA Consumer Duty evidence. They're complementary — Freespee tells you what happened, Cresta helps you improve it and prove compliance.

objections.objection: Our CC is too small for enterprise AI
objections.response: Over-50s specialist insurance with pre-existing conditions = very high value per call. The ROI calculation is about complexity and compliance, not just volume.

messages.subject: Manchester United fan services — you turned Manchester Airport into a best-in-class operation

Collette,

You turned Manchester Airport's customer operations into a best-in-class service engine — millions of passengers, emotional journey moments, premium experiences.

Manchester United has a similar challenge: millions of fan interactions, premium hospitality to upsell, and a brand where every conversation matters — whether it's a season ticket query or an Old Trafford hospitality booking.

Marcus Nolan, Cresta

objections.response: The commercial mandate under INEOS is exactly what makes this relevant. Fan services revenue is part of the commercial picture — and a well-managed CC is part of that.
